SEARCH

All Classes and Interfaces

Classes
Class
Description
AssetSetter
Class to place the various objects and monsters on the map
Bat
Class to model the behaviour of bat-monsters It extends the superclass Monster
Bomb
Class for the bomb objects that are placed by the bomber.
BombView
Class about the view-part of the bombs, to pass the position without the need for the model and the view to communicate
Character
Subclass of the Entity class, models the basic aspects of moving characters such as the player and the enemies
CollisionChecker
Class to check the collisions between the various elements of the game
DataStorage
Class with the main attributes to save and store
Entity
Class to model the general and basic aspects of an entity in this game, such as the position where to draw on the gamePanel or the collision
Explosion
Class to model the graphic part of the explosions
ExplosionHandler
Class to handle the collision of the explosion with the player and the monster
GameFrame
Class to build the frame to draw on the screen
GamePanel
Main class for the view, to handle the view of the game and drew the objects on the screen.
GameStateManager
Utility class to set and retrieve the current game state to use them in various parts of the project
Heart
Small class just to get the heart image to draw to represent lives
JBomberMan
Main class for the controller that handle the game loop
KeyHandler
Class to handle what to do every time a key is pressed
ModelAssets
This class contains useful arrays to keep track of the objects to manage.
Monster
Abstract class for the monsters and their general aspects
MonsterView
Class to manage the position of the monsters for the view package so that the package does not need to communicate with the model
NewProfileDialog
Class to create a new profile
PlayerModel
Class to model the model of the player.
PlayerView
Class to manage the position of the player for the view package
PowerUp
Class used just to keep track in the model of the powerups on the map.
PowerUpView
 
SaveLoad
Class to save and load the values in the DataStorage class
Slime
Class to model the behaviour of slime-monsters It extends the superclass Monster
Sound
Class to manage the sound effects and the music of the game
SuperObject
Abstract class with the basic aspects to keep track of powerups in general.
Tile
Class to represent a single tile of the game map
TileManager
Class to manage the loading of the maps used for the levels
UI
Class to draw all the user interface screens.
UtilityTool
Class with some useful utility tools
